title: Trump Is Finally Eyeing an Exit From Iran. But Will He Take It?
summary: President Trump says he is considering “winding down” operations in Iran. But many of his original war goals remain unaccomplished.

summary: There are several ways the United States could attempt to reopen the waterway, all of which carry substantial risks.

summary: The current restrictions on the internet in Iran are coinciding with Nowruz, the observance of the Persian New Year.

title: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Director Who Led Trump Inquiry, Dies at 81
summary: He imposed the most significant overhaul of the F.B.I. in its history. Later, after concluding that Russia had interfered in the 2016 election, he became a target of the president’s anger.

title: Trump’s Reaction to Mueller’s Death: ‘Good, I’m Glad.’
summary: The president has been known to disparage foes after their deaths. His response to Mr. Mueller’s death drew criticism from Republicans and Democrats.

title: What Was the Mueller Investigation? And What Did It Find?
summary: The appointment of Robert S. Mueller III as special counsel to conduct an investigation related to a sitting president roiled Washington around the start of President Trump’s first term.

summary: Mr. Beshear, the Democratic governor of Kentucky, sharply criticized Mr. Vance as both angle for potential presidential runs.

title: ICE Agents to Be Sent to U.S. Airports on Monday, Trump Says
summary: The president issued a threat to deploy ICE agents in an apparent attempt to force Democrats to approve a new budget for the Department of Homeland Security.

title: How Corey Lewandowski Wielded Power Inside D.H.S.
summary: While he was supposed to serve in a limited role as an adviser to Kristi Noem, Mr. Lewandowski had wide influence over contracts, personnel and operations inside the agency over the last year.

title: The Billionaire Funding France’s Far Right
summary: Pierre-Édouard Stérin is financing projects to make France less Muslim, more Catholic and more capitalist. He says his program has trained thousands running for municipal office on Sunday.

title: France Is Voting for Thousands of New Mayors. Here’s Why It Matters.
summary: The far-right party had first-round successes in several cities, but so did the far left. Sunday’s second round may clarify who has real momentum.

title: CBS News Radio Crackles to a Close
summary: A stalwart of the mass media century, the longstanding network that launched the careers of journalists like Edward R. Murrow will air its final broadcast in May.

summary: America shouldn’t fight a war like this.

summary: What a 40-year-old movie told me about childhood today.

title: Italy’s Leader Wants to Change the Constitution. Italians Don’t Get It.
summary: Voting starts Sunday on a referendum to overhaul Italy’s judiciary. Many people don’t understand the complex proposal — and it could hurt Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.

title: Maui Braces for Storm as Oahu Lifts Evacuation Orders
summary: Thousands of residents on Oahu were allowed to return home on Saturday afternoon as the storm moved to Maui.

title: Election in Slovenia Will Test Europe’s Political Mood
summary: The center-left government is being challenged by a right-wing populist accused of launching a smear campaign in the final days of a close race.
